title: bigelowia douglasii var. pumila
intro: Bigelowia douglasii var. pumila, also known as dwarf bigelowia, is a small shrub or perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family. It is native to the western United States and is used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, sore throats, and skin conditions, thanks to its ability to soothe and calm inflammation. Herbal preparations of Bigelowia douglasii var. pumila include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or using a solvent such as ethanol.
